Oklahoma Occupational Therapist License

Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure — OT
Renew on board site (opens in new tab)

Last updated March 22, 2026.

Become a Occupational Therapist in Oklahoma

Oklahoma Occupational Therapist license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the NBCOT exam, and completion of the application process.

Steps to Get Certified

Follow these steps to become a Occupational Therapist in Oklahoma.

  1. Step 1

    Complete an Accredited Occupational Therapy Program

    Graduate from an ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy educational program.

  2. Step 2

    Pass the NBCOT Exam

    Successfully complete the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination.

  3. Step 3

    Apply for a License

    Submit a completed application, required documentation, and fees to the Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision.

Out-of-State Occupational Therapists

Oklahoma has enacted the OT Compact, but the compact is not live for clinicians yet. For now, out-of-state applicants still use endorsement.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Oklahoma.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Oklahoma can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: Oklahoma requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: OT Compact is enacted in Oklahoma, but it is not issuing privileges yet.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

Oklahoma Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed occupational therapists applying to practice in Oklahoma.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure — OT. The application fee is N/A.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    Oklahoma requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Request Official Transcripts

    Sent directly from your educational institution.

  4. Step 4

    Submit Your NBCOT Score Report

    Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

  5. Step 5

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Oklahoma requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Oklahoma can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  6. Step 6

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

  7. Step 7

    Complete an Accredited Occupational Therapy Program

    Graduate from an ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy educational program.

  8. Step 8

    Apply for a License

    Submit a completed application, required documentation, and fees to the Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your Oklahoma Occupational Therapist license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
N/A
Background check
$0 (included in application) (The board's processing fee is included in the application, but out-of-state fingerprinting services charge a separate fee of ~$40)
Total estimated cost
$150 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
4 to 6 Weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Temporary License

Oklahoma offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Oklahoma Occupational Therapist License Renewal

Maintain your active license and stay compliant with the Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure — OT.

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$75

Oklahoma Board of Medical Licensure — OT

Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision

Oklahoma Occupational Therapist Licensing FAQ

How much does a Oklahoma OT license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

Is NBCOT certification required?

Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for occupational therapists?

Oklahoma has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Oklahoma?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
